steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The researchers warn that rising total seagrass coverage did not mean the ecosystem was resilient. The correct choice must show a scenario where coverage grew yet the system still collapsed under a different environmental stress.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — algal blooms block sunlight in early 2019: the graph shows all three lines dropping sharply in early 2019 after years of growth — a synchronized collapse that a light-blocking event would cause. ✓
  • Choice B — fungal infection in 2018: the graph shows no 2018 decline; widgeon grass keeps rising through late 2018. ✗
  • Choice C — nutrient runoff drove the 2012–2017 increase: explains the growth period, not the collapse. ✗
  • Choice D — only widgeon grass tolerates the 2019 temperature spike: widgeon grass coverage also drops in 2019, so it did not thrive. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
